reference: I suddenly felt some numbness on the left side of my face along the jawline. When I looked in the mirror, the left side of my face, below the jawline and more in the neck area, was swollen and felt like a lump there. I went to the urgent care center and they diagnosed it as a blockage in the carotid artery and told me to eat sour candy to reduce the swelling. Does this make sense?
text: I suddenly felt some numbness on the left side of my face along the jawline. When I looked in the mirror, the left side of my face below the jawline and more in the neck area was swollen, and felt like a lump there. I went to the urgent care center, and they diagnosed it as a blockage in the carotid artery, and told me to eat sour candy to reduce the swelling. Does this make sense?
